danger_signals.visual_form: Animated guard character moving toward player
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat of death; contact causes level restart and life loss

danger_signals.visual_form: Dug floor cavity, player standing at edge or falling into it
danger_signals.meaning: Fatal trap if player falls in before hole refills; guard death if trapped inside

danger_signals.visual_form: Guard sprite with gold bar visual attachment
danger_signals.meaning: Guard is a threat multiplier; if trapped with gold, creates level puzzle complexity

danger_signals.visual_form: Floor tiles with distinct visual marking
danger_signals.meaning: Uncontrollable fall hazard for both player and guards

danger_signals.signal: Guard spawning at top of level
danger_signals.visual_form: Guard respawn at screen top after being consumed in hole
danger_signals.meaning: Threat regeneration; guards return after environmental death

safety_signals.visual_form: Gold bar sprites scattered throughout level
safety_signals.meaning: Objective completion marker; collecting all gold is prerequisite for level exit
Safe zone and level completion point after collecting all gold

safety_signals.visual_form: Guard standing immobilized in dug pit as floor refills
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary safety window; guard unable to pursue during entrapment

safety_signals.visual_form: Player sprite positioned directly above guard sprite (feet-to-head contact)
safety_signals.meaning: Safe contact state; allows traversal over trapped guards without damage

safety_signals.visual_form: Vertical ladder graphics or suspended horizontal bars
safety_signals.meaning: Mobility and escape routes; multiple traversal paths reduce guard encounters

safety_signals.visual_form: Indestructible floor tiles with distinct visual pattern
safety_signals.meaning: Impassable barriers that create safe zones guards cannot breach via digging

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Gold collection trajectory; dug holes; cleared level paths

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Guard carrying gold bar appears distinct from unloaded guard
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Guard respawn points at top of screen; pursuit patterns; hole entrapment zones

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Gold bars visible on level platforms
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Guards actively patrol and pursue player; exit at top of screen is inaccessible

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Gold pieces vanish from level; exit path becomes active
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player can now reach screen top to advance; guard behavior continues but objective shifts

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Guard immobilized in dug pit; hole visibly refilling
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Guard ceases pursuit; becomes static obstacle or platform for player to traverse

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Guard sprite transitions below trapdoor tiles
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Guard falls uncontrollably until hitting floor or terrain; respawns at top if consumed

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Player sprite descending into dug pit with floor above refilling
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player death imminent if trapped; hole becomes impassable barrier

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Gold bar sprite attached to or carried by guard character
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Guard becomes higher-priority target for trapping; gold becomes retrievable after guard falls

environmental_storytelling.example: Gold placement patterns communicate puzzle intent; guards positioned to create combat/evasion paths
Trapdoors and bedrock position obstacles and shortcuts, telling story of dungeon layout without text
Guards always respawn at screen top after environmental death, establishing dungeon as cyclic threat system
Brick platform motif with ladders and bars creates visual language of vault/mine structure with escape routes
Guards sometimes repelled or move away despite player proximity; communicates they follow hidden rules, not simple pursuit

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Can walk, climb ladders, hang from bars, dig holes in adjacent tiles, traverse at variable speed
Cannot climb out; death imminent if hole fills; must escape before refill or lose life
Falls faster than guards; can sustain arbitrary height falls without injury; can use falling guards as bridges
Moves in search pattern that does not always follow shortest path to player; sometimes repelled; alternative movement
Limited to carrying one bar at a time; if trapped in hole, will drop gold upon entrapment
Immobilized until hole refills; cannot escape pit; consumed if refill completes before escape
Respawns immediately at random location at top of level; regeneration mechanic
Falls uncontrollably like player; respawns at top if falls into void

notes: Lode Runner communicates world-state primarily through environmental puzzle structure rather than narrative or faction tension. Danger is signaled mechanistically: guards and holes are direct threats with clear visual manifestations. Safety exists in level geometry (bedrock, exit) and strategic guard manipulation (trapping). The game's core loop involves reading guard AI patterns (counterintuitive movement tells) and timing holes/gold collection against pursuit. No political factions exist; the framework is adversarial (player vs. guards) without ideological content. Visual communication relies on sprite distinctiveness (guards vs. player) and environmental affordances (diggable vs. bedrock tiles, trapdoors, ladders). World-state changes are purely mechanical: gold present/absent, guards trapped/free/respawning, level complete/incomplete.
